Saw this $MELI interview a few months ago. Here is how the strategy is actually playing out. In the interview, the VP of Marketing at Mercado Pago explains that they offer 24 interest-free installments on Mercado Livre for people using their credit card. Most retailers can never do this. High interest rates in Brazil make long-term financing extremely expensive. Because Mercado Livre and Mercado Pago are the same company, they do not have to pay a middleman bank to finance the purchase. They share the same profit margin. This allows them to offer 24-month credit lines without destroying their unit economics.

They offer instant cashback paid out in Meli Dolar. This is a stable coin pegged to the US Dollar. Instead of giving you points that take a month to clear, they drop funds into your digital wallet the second you make a purchase. This strategy traps user liquidity inside their own ecosystem. It influences consumers to spend that money back on the Mercado Livre platform instead of transferring it to an external bank. It also hedges against local currency inflation, making the reward feel much more valuable to the consumer.

The most aggressive part of their strategy is buying the deposit account. In the video, the strategy was 120% of the CDI yield on savings to become the primary bank for Brazilians. In March 2026, it shows they are leaning into this even harder today. They currently offer 105% CDI on checking balances and 120% CDI on their savings pockets for Meli+ subscribers. They even recently ran promotional yields up to 140% of the CDI. They are purposely bleeding cash on these high yields to force consumers to drain their legacy bank accounts and move their money to Mercado Pago.

Why does all of this matter right now? Because Mercado Livre is in a brutal war for market share in Brazil. Shopee just hit R$70 billion in GMV in 2025 and captured a ~14% market share. Mercado Livre is still winning with a ~39% total market share, but they cannot always beat Shopee on the unit price of cheap items.

Because they cannot win a race to the bottom on price, Mercado Livre is using their fintech moat to lock customers in. They are fully leveraging their structural advantage in fintech compared to their competitors to completely change the way they shop. If a shopper has money in Meli Dolar, or relies on a 24 month interest-free credit line from Mercado Pago, they are inclined to buy from Mercado Livre. To defend this ecosystem further, Mercado Livre just announced a massive R$57 billion investment in Brazil for 2026. They are building 14 new distribution centers to speed up deliveries and fight off the competition.

MELI is a closed-loop financial system. They use subsidized banking yields to acquire LTV customers, instant dollar-pegged cashback to keep customers in the ecosystem, and favorable in-house credit for further lock-in.

$MELI Music is a calculated tool for user acquisition. The festival launched in last year. It was created in partnership with Global Talent Services, a division of $UMG (cc: @BillAckman). The core goal is to lock younger consumers into the MercadoLibre ecosystem. It bridges the gap between a digital app and physical culture.

The strategy works as a closed-loop flywheel. First, it builds a subscription moat. MercadoLibre uses Meli Music to drive its Meli+ program. Subscribers get early access to tickets. They also get exclusive cashback. This adds massive value to the subscription. People are far less likely to cancel Meli+ if it guarantees them access to major cultural events.

Next is the fintech engine. Fans must use the Mercado Pago app to get the best deals, buy tickets, or earn the cashback. This forces people to download the app and fund their accounts. Once the user has the digital wallet, Mercado Libre can sell them high-margin products. They offer personal loans and high-yield savings accounts. The concert ticket is just the bait.

Then, there is the digital expansion. Not everyone can fit into the physical venue. So, the company streams the entire festival live on Mercado Play. This is their free streaming service. This turns a physical event into a massive digital tool. Fans log into the app just to watch the show and stay inside the ecosystem.

There is the advertising revenue too. The event integrates Mercado Ads. MercadoLibre monetizes this large, highly engaged audience. They sell sponsorships and ad space to other brands. The festival basically pays for itself while capturing new users.

This strategy is scaling aggressively. The first shows in 2025 featured Trap and Pop artists like Matuê and Luísa Sonza. They were held at the Mercado Pago Hall for 6.5k people. In January they confirmed a massive expansion. The May 2026 edition is moving to the main field of the Mercado Livre Arena Pacaembu. It will hold over 20k people. It will feature massive Sertanejo stars like Ana Castela and Lauana Prado.

Meli Music is a very public strategy. But it is a brilliant business move. By subsidizing live music, MercadoLibre lowers its customer acquisition costs. It builds intense brand loyalty. It outmaneuvers competitors who only compete on cheap products and fast shipping.

Nesse mês passei por duas situações esquisitas no mercado pago. 1- Ganhei R$14 na lotofácil, aí dá pra eles depositarem essa quantia diretamente no mercado pago, e foi a opção q escolhi. Peguei o comprovante de pagamento da caixa. Passou uma semana, fui ver o extrato e não estava lá o valor. Entrei em contato com o mercado pago e só assim colocaram o valor na minha conta, não tinha aparecido transação nenhuma. 2- Apareceu uma compra no meu cartão virtual as 2 e poca da manhã, horário q estava dormindo, para o ifood. Entrei em contato com Ifood e lá não tinha tido compra alguma, já o mercado pago disse q o saldo tinha saído mas o estabelecimento ainda faltava finalizar o pagamento. Aí esse valor fica numa espécie de limbo, pq sai da sua conta e não caiu ainda na conta do estabelecimento. Falaram q eu tinha q esperar de 2 a 10 dias, mesmo eu alegando fraude. Aí tive que ir no reclame aqui. Agora, nunca passei problema parecido pelo Santander. Bem absurdo eu ser descontado de uma compra que não fiz, papo do sistema dele ter sido hackeado.
